items.title: Anthropic mapped Claude's values in the wild
items.oneliner: Anthropic analyzed 309,000 Claude chats and found personality shifts by language and context, making alignment look operational, not abstract.

items.title: Prime Intellect rebuilt its agent evaluation stack
items.oneliner: Prime Intellect split agent evals into taskset, harness, and runtime, which is the right abstraction for reproducible RL.

items.title: Grok Build reportedly over-collected private code
items.oneliner: A test found Grok Build uploading 5.1GB from a 12GB repo, which turns developer trust into a security review.

items.title: Codex encrypted sub-agent prompts by default
items.oneliner: Codex now encrypts MultiAgentV2 prompts, improving privacy but gutting local audit trails when teams need to debug agent behavior.

items.title: Model cloning fears are becoming policy pressure
items.oneliner: OpenAI and Anthropic warned Chinese firms are cloning frontier models, pushing model exfiltration from theory into national security.

items.title: Germany may narrow freedom of information access
items.oneliner: Germany plans to restrict FOIA requests to natural persons and raise fees, which would make public-interest AI scrutiny harder.

items.title: The CUDA moat is finally being challenged
items.oneliner: Spectral Compute is betting developers want CUDA portability, because Nvidia lock-in is now a board-level infrastructure risk.

items.title: x86 is adding another AI math path
items.oneliner: Intel's ACE extends AMX with FP8-focused outer-product instructions, signaling CPUs still want a seat at inference workloads.

items.title: JetBrains quietly shipped a graph database
items.oneliner: YouTrackDB promises O(1) link traversal without runtime joins, which could matter for graph-heavy internal tooling and knowledge systems.

items.title: Anthropic localized Claude pricing for India
items.oneliner: Anthropic started India pricing for Claude, a reminder that model adoption now depends as much on billing geography as benchmarks.
